name: chrM_genome
description: Mitochondrial chromosome (chrM): 16,569 bp circular double-stranded DNA molecule encoding 13 OXPHOS protein subunits, 22 tRNAs, 2 rRNAs (12S, 16S), and a non-coding regulatory D-loop containing OriH, OriL, and the two HV1/HV2 hypervariable regions used in forensics. The GRCh38 chrM sequence is the revised Cambridge Reference Sequence (rCRS, Andrews 1999). Maps at 100-10000 copies per somatic cell depending on tissue; heteroplasmy (multiple co-existing mtDNA sequences within a cell) is the rule rather than the exception. Nuclear mitochondrial DNA segments (NUMTs) on the nuclear chromosomes mimic chrM at lower identity and must be flagged separately to avoid false heteroplasmy calls.
expected_copy_count: 100-10000 copies per somatic cell; varies by tissue (muscle and brain at the high end, mature blood cells lower); oocytes >100000

expected_paralogs.name: D_loop_HV1_HV2
expected_paralogs.note: non-coding control region 16024-576 spanning the origin
